hook.text: In 1994, the Standish Group reported that only 16% of software projects succeeded.

buildup.text: 31% of projects were cancelled outright. 53% cost nearly double their estimates.

discovery.text: Top causes: changing requirements, unrealistic timelines, and poor communication.

twist.text: Critics challenged the methodology — but the core message stuck: software projects fail a lot.

climax.text: The CHAOS Report became the rallying cry for a new way of building software.

punchline.text: One report scared an entire industry into changing.

quiz.question: According to the 1994 CHAOS Report, what percentage of software projects succeeded?
